suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: product category enum values ('Leather Goods', 'Jewelry', 'Watches', 'Fashion', 'Accessories', 'Other') should be defined as a shared constant (either in @galileo/shared or a common products constants file) rather than duplicated inline in route handlers. This prevents divergence when categories change.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature api-security-hardening duplicated PRODUCT_CATEGORIES array identically in apps/api/src/routes/products/create.ts:11-18 and apps/api/src/routes/products/update.ts:5-12.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: the brandId null guard pattern (check user.role !== 'ADMIN' && !user.brandId → 403) should be extracted into a reusable Fastify preHandler or middleware (similar to requireRole()) to reduce duplication and prevent omission in future routes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature api-security-hardening added identical brandId null guard blocks to 6 separate route files (list.ts, create.ts, get.ts, update.ts, mint.ts, qr.ts). The existing requireRole() middleware shows the project already uses this pattern for cross-cutting concerns.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: resolver route should import and use generateDid() and generateDigitalLinkUrl() from @galileo/shared instead of inline-constructing DID and Digital Link strings. This ensures consistency if the URI format changes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature api-csrf-resolver-conformity inline-constructs DID ('did:galileo:01:${gtin14}:21:${serial}') and digitalLink ('https://id.galileoprotocol.io/01/${gtin14}/21/...') in resolve.ts:68-74, while identical logic exists in packages/shared/src/validation/did.ts via generateDid() and generateDigitalLinkUrl().

rejectedObservations.observation: MintError ad-hoc error class pattern in mint.ts is undocumented, unlike direct reply.status().send() used elsewhere.
rejectedObservations.reason: Too minor and context-specific. The MintError class is a reasonable pattern for throwing from within Prisma $transaction callbacks. It doesn't warrant a convention as it's an implementation detail.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker followed backend-worker skill procedure with no deviations.
rejectedObservations.reason: Informational only — confirms skill procedure is adequate. No action needed.

rejectedObservations.observation: AGENTS.md already documents CSRF, resolver JSON-LD, and GTIN padding conventions comprehensively. Implementation matches exactly.
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented — confirms AGENTS.md guidance is accurate and being followed.
